title: Task Parallelization Headless Execution Plan
goal: Enable an explicit experimental headless parallel-step path that leases multiple safe task-lane jobs, executes providers concurrently, and commits through the single-writer run lock.
non_goals: Do not make parallel mode the default.
Do not remove or weaken sequential step.
Do not implement git worktree-based isolation in this slice.
Do not implement automatic merge conflict resolution in this slice.
Do not open visible worker terminals.
Do not infer safety from changed_files alone.

tasks.title: CLI command surface and gating
tasks.goal: Add explicit parent and internal worker commands while keeping ordinary step sequential.
acceptance: step remains sequential.
parallel-step only dispatches task-scoped phases.
parallel-step requires task_lane.mode=parallel and max_parallel_jobs > 1.
parallel-step returns a clear wait result when no candidates are available.
run-leased-job requires -JobPackageFile.
Test-ParallelStepLaunchableCandidate owns shared launchability rules and contains no launcher or commit behavior.

tasks.title: Batch lease and job package creation
tasks.goal: Derive batch lease candidates under one run lock and persist one executable package per leased job.
acceptance: Candidate selection uses Get-BatchLeaseCandidates.
Lease metadata preserves resource_locks, parallel_safety, slot_id, and workspace_id.
Each lease emits job.leased and task selection events.
Each package includes job spec, prompt text, phase definition, task id, phase, run id, and phase started timestamp.
Packages are written under the run job directory.
Each package records a workspace baseline before provider execution.
Each package records declared task changed_files and normalized allowed product paths.
New-ParallelStepJobPackages owns candidate filtering, lease creation, prompt rendering, baseline capture, and package persistence without launching providers.

tasks.title: Worker execution command
tasks.goal: Execute one pre-leased job package outside the run lock and commit under a short lock.
acceptance: Provider execution happens before acquiring the commit lock.
Worker acquires run lock in the pre-commit guard and holds it through artifact commit, state mutation, event append, and lease clear.
Commit fencing rereads latest state under the run lock and validates job_id, lease_token, phase, task_id, lease expiry, lease presence, and state_revision compatibility.
Commit rejection emits job.commit_rejected and exits nonzero.
Successful worker completion emits essential sequential-equivalent events.
Before commit, the worker compares actual workspace changes against the package baseline and declared changed_files.
Boundary drift rejects commit.
When a worker result requests approval, it sets task_lane.stop_leasing=true.
If another approval is already pending, a second approval commit is rejected or blocked with a clear approval.commit_rejected or job.commit_rejected event.
Invoke-LeasedJobPackage owns worker execution and commit; it does not rewrite sequential Invoke-EngineStep.

tasks.title: Parent worker launcher
tasks.goal: Launch one headless worker process per package and summarize results.
acceptance: Parent launches hidden worker windows on Windows.
Each worker has separate stdout/stderr log files.
Parent waits for all workers and returns JSON with job ids, task ids, package paths, exit codes, and log paths.
Parent does not hold run lock while workers execute.
Worker failures do not cause additional leases in the same invocation.
Parent emits or returns enough timing data to prove worker overlap in focused tests.
Invoke-ParallelStepWorkers consumes packages and never mutates run state directly.

tasks.title: Isolated workspace and serial merge-back guard
tasks.goal: Execute providers in per-job isolated copy workspaces and merge accepted files back serially under the run lock.
acceptance: parallel-step launches only candidates whose parallel_safety is parallel by default.
Candidates with empty declared changed_files are not launched by default.
cautious and serial candidates are not co-dispatched by default.
If fewer than two launchable candidates remain, parallel-step waits unless -AllowSingleParallelJob is passed.
Command output labels workspace mode as isolated-copy-experimental.

Workspace preparation copies the configured project workspace to a per-job directory under runs/<run-id>/workspaces/<job-id> while excluding heavy/runtime paths such as .git, node_modules, .next, coverage, and relay-dev runtime outputs.
Provider WorkingDirectory is the isolated workspace.
Relay-dev control-plane artifact paths remain the canonical main run paths from the prompt package.
Baseline capture and actual diff detection run inside the isolated workspace so sibling worker edits cannot appear in a job diff.
Actual isolated workspace changes outside declared task changed_files reject merge-back and commit.
Main workspace target files are compared against the package baseline immediately before merge-back; changed targets reject merge-back with a clear conflict event.
Merge-back copies only declared and accepted changed files from isolated workspace to main workspace under the run lock.
Actual product changes to known shared files reject merge-back unless protected by a matching resource_locks policy.

New-IsolatedJobWorkspace, New-WorkspaceBaselineSnapshot, Test-WorkspaceBoundaryDelta, and Invoke-IsolatedWorkspaceMergeBack are callable by Invoke-LeasedJobPackage without depending on launcher behavior.

tasks.title: Lease heartbeat and expiry policy
tasks.goal: Keep leases fresh while workers execute outside the run lock.
acceptance: Worker package execution starts a lightweight heartbeat loop or periodic heartbeat update process.
Heartbeat updates active_jobs[job_id].last_heartbeat_at and extends lease_expires_at under short run locks.
Heartbeat validates the current lease token before writing.
Stale recovery does not clear jobs with fresh heartbeat.
If heartbeat cannot update, worker execution may continue but commit fencing can reject it with a clear event.
Focused tests cover fresh heartbeat preservation and expired lease commit rejection.

tasks.title: Focused regression coverage
tasks.goal: Prove the new path actually runs workers concurrently while preserving existing sequential behavior.
acceptance: Two independent parallel tasks are leased and worker processes are launched.
Parent does not keep run.lock held while workers execute.
Worker commit clears its own lease and preserves other live leases until they commit.
Sequential step still dispatches only one job.
parallel-step refuses non-parallel lane mode.
parallel-step refuses cautious/serial co-dispatch by default.
Stale lease recovery still works after partial worker failure.
Isolated workspace boundary drift rejects merge-back and commit.
Main workspace drift on a declared target rejects merge-back.
Heartbeat keeps live long-running workers from being recovered as stale.
A second approval request is rejected or blocked when one approval is already pending.

worker_split.notes: Own Invoke-LeasedJobPackage and short-lock commit path.
Own Invoke-ParallelStepWorkers, isolated workspace helpers, merge-back helpers, and result summary. Call Worker A's launchability helper rather than redefining gating.
Own heartbeat state helpers and stale recovery integration.
Parent writes final tests or integrates worker-provided test hooks and runs all verification.

global_acceptance: parallel-step can actually run at least two fake-provider task-lane jobs concurrently.
The run lock is not held during provider execution.
Isolated copy workspace execution is used for provider concurrency.
Serial merge-back rejects undeclared isolated changes and main workspace target drift.
Heartbeat prevents live long-running workers from being stale-recovered.
Single pending_approval safety is preserved by lane stop and second-approval rejection/blocking.
Worklog records the explicit experimental status.

risks.title: Workspace merge-back conflicts
risks.mitigation: Use isolated copy workspaces for provider execution and reject merge-back when main target files drift.

risks.title: Duplicate or stale commit
risks.mitigation: Acquire run lock in pre-commit guard and hold it through commit/state/event updates; verify latest lease fencing.

risks.title: Event/state divergence
risks.mitigation: Mirror sequential post-transaction mutation/event behavior rather than creating a second transition model.

risks.title: Repair flow writes while unlocked
risks.mitigation: Keep repair semantics narrow in MVP; avoid broad parallel repair behavior unless covered by focused tests.

risks.title: Approval collision
risks.mitigation: Use lane stop and reject/block second approval commits until pending_approvals exists.
